channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitics headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳 China blocks Meta acquisition of AI startup Manus
item.description: Beijing has prohibited Meta Platforms USD 2 billion acquisition of Chinese-founded agentic AI startup Manus after a national security review by the National Development and Reform Commission, blocking the deal on tech transfer grounds.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US sanctions hit Chinese refiners linked to Iranian oil trade
item.description: US sanctions against Hengli Petrochemical over alleged Iranian crude purchases triggered a sharp 10% stock drop. China's largest independent refiner denied the allegations, saying it has never engaged in any trade with Iran.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China delivers record LNG carrier challenging South Korean shipbuilding dominance
item.description: China Merchants Heavy Industry delivered the 180,000-cubic-meter Celsius Georgetown, marking the fifth Chinese yard capable of building advanced LNG carriers as Beijing intensifies competition with Seoul in high-value shipbuilding.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran FM blames US for failed talks after arriving in Russia
item.description: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blamed Washington's excessive demands for the collapse of Islamabad negotiations, arriving in Saint Petersburg to meet Putin. Iran passed written red lines to Americans via Pakistan including nuclear and Hormuz issues.

item.title: 🏛️ Iran war deadline triggers Trump-Congress showdown
item.description: The 60-day limit under the War Powers Resolution for military operations without congressional approval expires May 1, forcing Trump to seek authorization or find alternative ways to legitimize the Iran operation as diplomatic efforts continue.

item.title: 🤖 DeepSeek releases new AI model to muted market response
item.description: Chinese startup DeepSeek previewed its next-generation AI model, but market reaction was subdued compared to last year's breakthrough. The fast-moving AI industry continues to evolve rapidly.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali defense minister killed in insurgent assault
item.description: Mali's Defense Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks by insurgents including al Qaeda's West Africa affiliate, marking a major blow to the government.

item.title: 🇹🇩 At least 42 killed in Chad water resource clashes
item.description: A clash between two families over a water point in eastern Chad escalated into reprisal attacks, killing at least 42 people amid growing resource scarcity in the Sahel region.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel issues evacuation warning for seven Lebanese towns
item.description: The Israeli military warned residents of seven Lebanese towns north of the Litani River to evacuate, signaling potential military operations near the border with Lebanon.

item.title: ⚔️ Global military spending rises 2.9% despite US decline
item.description: World military expenditure increased in 2025 even as US spending declined, driven by ongoing conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East as well as rising tensions in Asia.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran war continues as Hormuz remains blocked
item.description: The US-Iran conflict continues with the Strait of Hormuz still blocked. Diplomatic efforts through Pakistan and Oman have stalled as both sides maintain positions.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian drone attack on Odesa wounds 10
item.description: A Russian drone strike on Odesa wounded 10 people including children, as attacks on Ukrainian port infrastructure continue.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran FM heads to Russia as Trump says call to negotiate
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi traveled to Russia for talks with Putin as diplomatic channels remain active. Trump said Iran can call the US if they want to negotiate.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu rivals join forces against him
item.description: Former PM Naftali Bennett and current opposition leader Yair Lapid announced a joint electoral list to challenge Benjamin Netanyahu in Israel's next election.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Araghchi visits Russia as US talks on hold
item.description: Iran's foreign minister is in Russia for talks with Putin, with the diplomat also visiting Oman and Pakistan as regional powers seek to mediate between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey offers support after WHCD shooting
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan offered support to Trump in a phone call following the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea and Russia strengthen anti-West alliance
item.description: Kim Jong-un and Putin are building a powerful bulwark against the West as the Ukraine war continues, deepening military cooperation between Moscow and Pyongyang.

item.title: 🛰️ Indian firm develops satellite missile detection
item.description: An Indian startup is developing space-based thermal observation systems to detect missile launches in hotspots, adding to regional early warning capabilities.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German activists raid Elbit Systems
item.description: German activists targeted Israeli defense contractor Elbit Systems in a raid, amid growing protests against arms exports to Israel.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel activists raid Elbit Systems facility
item.description: German activists targeted Israeli defense contractor Elbit Systems in a raid, amid growing protests against arms exports to Israel.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Netanyahu rivals join forces ahead of election
item.description: Former Israeli PM Naftali Bennett and opposition leader Yair Lapid are forming a joint electoral list to challenge Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles US trip to proceed despite shooting
item.description: Buckingham Palace confirmed King Charles' upcoming US trip will proceed following the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, as the monarch seeks to repair US-UK relations.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran offers proposal to reopen Hormuz via Pakistan
item.description: Iran submitted a new proposal through Pakistani mediators to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war while postponing nuclear talks to a later stage.
